_note: Built-in split functions. Reference by name in rule files.

two_offset.description: For each attr1 value at index i, include (i, i%n_a2) and (i, (i+1)%n_a2) in source. Guarantees bipartite connectivity. Gen = all remaining pairs. A seed parameter controls the random permutation of attribute orderings before applying the formula.

params.seed: int (default 0) — controls permutation of attribute orderings

conditional_two_offset.description: Regime-aware two-offset split for conditional tasks. Groups attr1 values by regime (from comp_dict["selector"]["map"]) and applies a within-regime two-offset cover. Guarantees each attr1 value has ≥ 2 distinct attr2 partners (regime identification) and every attr2 value appears within each regime (within-regime mapping identification). Precondition: n_regime_classes >= n_a2 - 1 per regime.

params.seed: int (default 0) — controls permutation of role ordering

c4_override.description: Cover all (n_a1-1) classes fully in source; give the held-out class exactly one non-override demo. Gen contains the held-out class with the override value (tests override transfer) and the held-out class with remaining unseen non-override values (tests per-value specificity).

params.seed: int (optional) — shuffles class ordering to permute which class is held out

explicit.description: User-specified source and gen pairs as lists of [attr1_idx, attr2_idx] pairs.
